title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week focused on making OpsPal more reliable and self-healing. The platform now automatically cleans up old files and fixes common setup issues behind the scenes, while Salesforce users will see fewer errors when creating record types and working with Dynamic Forms on pages.

entries.headline: Platform automatically cleans up old files during updates
details: Removes outdated cache files older than 7 days
Cleans up abandoned session files older than 5 minutes
Alerts you when memory usage reports get too large

entries.headline: Platform removes outdated add-ons automatically
details: Works across Windows, Mac, and Linux systems
Detects and removes any outdated marketplace add-ons
Keeps your plugin library clean and current

entries.headline: Better startup reliability for Salesforce connections
details: Removes outdated data quality tools from background processes
Migrates old audit configurations to new format
Adds quality and architecture auditing for Salesforce orgs

entries.headline: Salesforce form and record type management works more reliably
details: Prevents invisible field additions when Dynamic Forms are active on pages
Automatically assigns new record types to System Administrator profile
Reduces session interruptions from routing conflicts

entries.headline: Platform automatically fixes missing dependencies at startup
details: Detects and installs missing required components
Shows clear error messages when installations fail
Runs fixes in the background to avoid delays

entries.headline: Eliminated false missing package errors at startup
